title: Trump administration appeals ruling that blocked Pentagon action against Anthropic over AI dispute

title: Is X down? Thousands of users report issues with news feed in US: What we know
snippet: Elon Musk's platform X is reportedly facing another outage, preventing users from accessing feeds. As per users, issues include login failures and app crashes, marking the third disruption.&nbsp;

title: Realme 16 with iPhone Air like camera module and 7,000mAh battery launched in India, price starts at  ₹31,999
snippet: Realme has launched the Realme 16 in India with a 120Hz AMOLED display, 7,000mAh battery, and MediaTek Dimensity 6400 Turbo processor. The phone is at  ₹31,999 and it will be available from April 5&nbsp;&nbsp;

title: Vivo V70 FE with 200MP camera and 7,000mAh battery launched in India, price starts at  ₹37,999
snippet: Vivo has launched the V70 FE in India with a 1.5K AMOLED display, MediaTek Dimensity 7360 Turbo processor, and IP68/IP69 ratings. The phone starts at a price of  ₹37,999 and will be available for pre-order today&nbsp;

title: What is OpenAI’s ‘Spud’? Greg Brockman teases new ChatGPT model built on years of research
snippet: OpenAI's Greg Brockman has announced that the company's upcoming AI model ‘Spud’ is a result of two years of research. The new model will serve as a foundational base model for future ChatGPT iterations

title: Redmi Note 15 SE 5G with 6.77-inch AMOLED display and 5,800 mAH battery launched in India, price starts at…
snippet: Xiaomi has launched the budget-friendly Redmi Note 15 SE 5G with a 120Hz AMOLED display, Snapdragon 6 Gen 3 processor, and military-grade durability. The phone starts at a price of  ₹19,999 and will be available from April 7&nbsp;

title: ChatGPT and Anthropic could soon route extremist AI users to a new third-party tool: Report
snippet: A new tool being developed in New Zealand aims to direct individuals exhibiting violent extremist tendencies on ChatGPT to deradicalization support. The tool is being created by a company called ThroughLine.

title: Meta warns iPhone users of Italian spyware campaign disguised as WhatsApp: Here's what you need to know
snippet: WhatsApp has alerted around 200 users in Italy about a malicious version of the app containing spyware developed by SIO. Victims were tricked into downloading the fake app through social engineering techniques.

title: Before you pick the MacBook Neo take a look at these alternatives that may suit you better

snippet: Microsoft will invest ten billion dollars in Japan from 2026 to 2029. This investment will expand artificial intelligence infrastructure and boost cybersecurity cooperation. title: Database startup Supabase seeks to double valuation to $10 billion: Report
snippet: Singapore sovereign wealth fund ​GIC is in ​talks to lead an investment ​in open-source database startup Supabase at a valuation of about $10 billion, The Information reported on ‌Thursday.

title: Under global spotlight, Australia plays hardball on social media ban
snippet: The global spotlight on the first ​such ban on people under 16 using popular apps appears to have encouraged Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's government to ramp up enforcement ​this week of what it was touting as successful cooperation with industry two months ago, tech policy experts say.

title: BigBasket, 1mg founders plan transition; QED's Morris flags credit tightening
snippet: Happy Friday! BigBasket and 1mg founders are preparing to step back from day-to-day roles. This and more in today's ETtech Morning Dispatch.

title: Product design startup Noon emerges from stealth mode with $44 million funding
snippet: San Francisco-headquartered startup Noon has emerged from stealth mode with $44 million in funding from Chemistry, First Round Capital, Scribble Ventures, Elevation Capital, and Afore Capital, along with individual investors from the technology and design industries.

title: Crisis contractor for OpenAI, Anthropic eyes a move to combat extremism
snippet: People who show violent extremist tendencies on ChatGPT will be directed to human and chatbot‑based deradicalisation support through a new tool in development ​in New Zealand, the people behind it said.

title: Powerus backed by Trump sons looks to sell drone interceptors to Gulf states being attacked by Iran
snippet: Powerus cofounder Brett Velicovich told The Associated Press that the company is making sales pitches that include drone demonstrations in several Gulf countries to show how its defensive drone interceptors could help them ward off Iranian attacks.

title: Broadcom taps Alphabet executive Amie Thuener as next CFO
snippet: US ​chip designer Broadcom on ​Thursday named Alphabet executive Amie Thuener ​as its next finance chief, effective June 12, when incumbent Kirsten Spears retires. Thuener is currently Alphabet's vice president, corporate controller and ‌chief ⁠accounting officer.

title: Small cities like Mangalore, Bhopal, Aurangabad join GCC race
snippet: Tier-2 cities now make up 30-32% of all new job postings, according to Sachin Alug, CEO of talent solutions firm NLB Services. “Hiring demand for tech and GCC roles in cities like Mangalore, Ahmedabad, and Nagpur has picked up noticeably, driven by the rise of nano-GCCs,” he said.
